name: ReAct and Reasoning-Action Loops
description: Interleaving reasoning traces (Thought) with grounded actions (Act) and observations (Observe) to enable LLMs to solve multi-step tasks with external grounding. The reasoning trace provides interpretability and error-correction opportunities mid-task. Extensions like Reflexion and Tree-of-Thought build on this foundation to improve long-horizon reliability.
contrarian_take: ReAct-style reasoning traces look interpretable but the trace is frequently post-hoc rationalization rather than actual planning — the model commits to an action before the trace is complete. The real research frontier is work that empirically tests whether reasoning traces causally improve accuracy vs. just correlate with correct outcomes. Most papers do not make this distinction.
example_signals: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
